title: Welcome to Claw3D
description: A guided tour through Claw3D, the 3D workspace and visualization layer for AI agents powered by OpenClaw.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of AI agents and automation
Familiarity with Next.js, React, and TypeScript

steps.title: Welcome to Claw3D
steps.description:
Claw3D is an open-source 3D workspace where AI agents collaborate visually. Agents review code, run tests, and execute tasks inside a shared 3D office environment. Think of it as a virtual office for your AI team.

The project is a Next.js application with a Phaser-based 3D rendering engine, a WebSocket gateway proxy, and a rich agent management system. OpenClaw provides the intelligence underneath.

interactiveActions.description: Click on README.md to see the full project overview

steps.title: Next.js Application Shell
steps.description: The app is built on Next.js using the App Router. The home screen and root layout live at the top level of the app directory. From there, the main routes branch into the 3D office workspace, the agent fleet management and settings screens, and the server-side API routes that handle communication with the gateway, office state, and studio configuration.

interactiveActions.description: Click on page.tsx to see the home screen

steps.title: Agent Management System
steps.description:
The agent system is the heart of Claw3D. It manages the full lifecycle of AI agents, from creation and provisioning through to runtime operations and state management.

The components layer provides the user interface for agent chat, creation, inspection, skills, and the fleet sidebar. The approval system enforces human oversight through execution approval workflows with control loops and pause policies. Hooks and state management tie everything together.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over the approvals directory to see the execution approval workflows

steps.title: The 3D Office
steps.description:
The visual centerpiece of Claw3D is a Phaser-powered 3D office where agents sit at desks and collaborate. The rendering engine handles scenes, objects, overlays, and navigation throughout the space.

The office includes UI panels for analytics, inbox, playbooks, and a skills marketplace. There is also a retro-themed variant with its own furniture geometry and desk navigation routes. All 3D furniture models like desks, chairs, computers, and plants are stored as assets in the public directory.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over the phaser directory to see the 3D rendering system

steps.title: Gateway Proxy and Server
steps.description:
The browser never talks directly to the OpenClaw Gateway. Everything flows through the Studio proxy for security and deployment flexibility.

The server layer is an Express application that handles WebSocket forwarding, authentication gating, and network policy enforcement. On the client side, a gateway abstraction manages the connection and error handling. The API routes provide endpoints for agent state, media, and skills.

interactiveActions.description: Click on gateway-proxy.js to see the WebSocket proxy

steps.title: Shared Libraries and Utilities
steps.description:
The shared libraries are the connective tissue that links the features together. The office library handles state management, presence tracking, standups, and event triggers. The gateway library manages the client connection to OpenClaw.

There are also dedicated libraries for the skill system, agent avatars, and scheduled tasks. The security library handles permission sandboxing to keep agent actions safe and controlled.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over the office library to see state management and event systems

steps.title: Testing and Developer Tooling
steps.description:
Claw3D uses a multi-layer testing strategy. Unit tests are powered by Vitest, and end-to-end tests run through Playwright to verify the full user experience.

The scripts directory contains developer helpers for cleanup, smoke testing the dev server, and syncing with the OpenClaw gateway. An environment variable template is included to help new contributors get their local setup running quickly.

interactiveActions.description: Click on .env.example to see the required environment variables

steps.title: What's Next
steps.description:
You've explored the full Claw3D codebase. From here, the README has a quick start guide and project overview. The Architecture guide covers system boundaries and data flow. The Roadmap shows current priorities and good first contributions. The Contributing guide explains local setup and PR expectations.

There is also detailed code documentation and a step-by-step tutorial available in the repository. The project is early-stage and actively evolving. Check GitHub Issues for current work. Welcome to the team!

interactiveActions.description: Explore the full codebase on your own
